entries.title: 1.9L Diesel

Injector seal leaks leading to fuel odour
Turbo actuator sticking on high‑mileage examples

Timing chain tensioner wear on later examples

commonProblems.h2: Vauxhall Signum Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Vauxhall Signum Timing Chain Failure - Why Z22SE Petrol Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Signum Life, Design, Elite, Exclusiv (2003-2008, Z22SE 2.2L petrol)
problems.rootCause: Factory timing chain tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing chain slack that causes rattling, misfires, and potential valve damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides, and chain if stretched; reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before rattling starts; 40k-60k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt Z22SE with upgraded tensioner kit, bench tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high‑mileage Signum; resolves root cause permanently

problems.recommendation: If your Z22SE Signum is under 70k miles and showing early tensioner noise, a preventative tensioner kit from a specialist offers good value. For engines over 90k miles or with confirmed chain stretch, a rebuilt unit with upgraded components is more cost‑effective than risking catastrophic failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Z22SE engine prices for your Vauxhall Signum

problems.h4: Vauxhall Signum EGR Valve Failure - Why Z19DTH Diesel Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Signum Design, Elite, Exclusiv (2005-2008, Z19DTH 1.9L CDTi)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valve and cooler become clogged with carbon deposits, restricting airflow and triggering limp mode or warning lights.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR valve and cooler, clean intake manifold, reset fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; clogging often recurs within 20k miles on short‑journey cars

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt Z19DTH with emissions‑compliant EGR system, DPF health check, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long‑term solution for ULEZ compliance and reliability on high‑mileage Signum

problems.recommendation: For Signum owners in ULEZ/CAZ zones, investing in a rebuilt Z19DTH with verified emissions compliance avoids repeat EGR repairs and daily charges. If the car is used mainly for short trips, consider whether replacement economics justify repair costs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Z19DTH Vauxhall Signum diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Vauxhall Signum Oil Pump Failure - Why Z18XE Petrol Engines Seize
problems.rootCause: Oil pump pickup strainer clogs with sludge, starving bearings of lubrication and causing low oil pressure warnings followed by catastrophic failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil pump, clean/replace pickup strainer, flush lubrication system, new oil/filter
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before bearing damage; requires immediate attention at first warning

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt Z18XE with upgraded oil pump components, pressure tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for any Signum showing oil pressure warnings; prevents repeat failure

problems.recommendation: Oil pressure warnings on a Z18XE Signum require immediate specialist inspection. If bearing wear is confirmed, replacement is the only safe option. For preventative maintenance on lower‑mileage examples, a specialist oil system service with upgraded pickup strainer can extend life.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt Z18XE engine options for your Vauxhall Signum

problems.h4: Vauxhall Signum Turbo Actuator Failure - Why VXR Z28NET Engines Lose Boost
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Signum VXR (2007-2008, Z28NET/Z28NEL 2.8L V6 turbo)
problems.rootCause: Turbo wastegate actuator calibration drifts or sticks, causing inconsistent boost pressure, limp mode, or complete loss of performance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace actuator, recalibrate wastegate via Tech2, dyno test boost curve
repairOptions.longevity: May resolve issue if turbo core is healthy; calibration expertise critical

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt Z28NET/Z28NEL with verified turbo assembly, performance calibration, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option for VXR owners seeking restored performance and reliability

problems.recommendation: VXR Signum owners should seek a specialist familiar with Z28NET calibration. If the turbo core is intact, actuator replacement may suffice. For engines with additional wear or high mileage, a performance‑verified rebuilt unit offers peace of mind and retained value.
problems.cta: Compare VXR Z28NET engine rebuild quotes for your Signum

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Vauxhall Signum
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Signum suffers timing chain rattle, EGR clogging, oil pressure warnings, or turbo issues, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the known failure point, bench testing, and a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. With nationwide supply & fit available, you can get back on the road without paying main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Vauxhall Signum Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Vauxhall Signum and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Vauxhall Signum Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: Vauxhall Signum 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L CDTi diesel powered most Signum variants from launch to end of production, appearing in Life, Design and Elite trims. This versatile unit offered strong fuel economy but requires attention to EGR and DPF maintenance on post‑2005 Euro 4 models.
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall Signum 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Vauxhall Signum 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The larger 2.2L CDTi diesel was offered in Design and Elite trims from 2005, providing extra torque for towing. Less common than the 1.9L but sought after for its pulling power, though turbo and injector issues require specialist attention.
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall Signum 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Vauxhall Signum 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The rare 3.0L V6 CDTi diesel was exclusive to top‑spec Elite models from 2005-2008. A complex twin‑turbo unit offering smooth power delivery but demanding specialist rebuilding due to its intricate fuel and turbo systems.
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall Signum 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Vauxhall Signum 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry‑level 1.8L naturally aspirated petrol engine powered early Signum Life models. Simple and affordable to replace but prone to oil pump pickup clogging, making regular oil changes critical for longevity.
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall Signum 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Vauxhall Signum 2.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most common Signum petrol engine, the 2.2L Z22SE powered Life, Design, Elite and Exclusiv trims throughout production. Robust but infamous for timing chain tensioner failure, making upgraded replacement kits essential.
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall Signum 2.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Vauxhall Signum 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The turbocharged 2.0L Z20NET petrol engine was offered in Design trim, delivering strong performance but requiring careful maintenance of its direct injection system to prevent carbon buildup on inlet valves.
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall Signum 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Vauxhall Signum 2.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship 2.8L V6 turbo petrol engine powered the rare Signum VXR from 2007-2008. A high‑performance unit requiring specialist calibration and rebuilding, with turbo actuator and bearing wear being key concerns. Not sure which engine size your Vauxhall Signum needs? engineYears.h2: Vauxhall Signum Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major Vauxhall Signum engine changes from 2003 launch to 2008 end of production, helping you identify your exact engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Signum launched on the Epsilon platform sharing with Vectra C, offering 1.8L petrol and 1.9L CDTi diesel engines across Life and Design trims.
keyChanges: New "saloon-estate" body style introduced to UK market
Initial engine lineup: Z18XE 1.8L petrol, Z19DT 1.9L CDTi diesel
Life and Design trim levels available from launch
knownFor: Unique rear seat chassis requires specialist fitting knowledge
Z19DT EGR valve clogging emerges as common high‑mileage issue
Z22SE timing chain tensioner wear begins appearing on early examples

years.preview: Euro 4 Update: Z19DTH & Elite Trim Arrive
years.description: The 2005 facelift introduced Euro 4 compliant Z19DTH diesel and the premium Elite trim, alongside the rare 3.0L V6 diesel option.
keyChanges: Euro 4 emissions compliance introduced across diesel range
Z19DTH 1.9L CDTi with DPF replaces Z19DT in Elite/Design
Elite trim launched with enhanced equipment and engine options
3.0L V6 CDTi (Y30DT) added to Elite range
knownFor: Z19DTH DPF regeneration failures become common on short‑journey cars
Y30DT V6 diesel complexity demands specialist rebuilding expertise
Timing chain tensioner issues on Z22SE now well‑documented by Vauxhall TSBs

years.description: The Signum range concluded with the high‑performance VXR variant featuring the 2.8L V6 turbo, alongside final‑year updates to diesel emissions calibration.

Final production year 2008; no direct successor model
Updated ECU calibration for Z19DTH to improve DPF regeneration
Exclusiv trim discontinued, Elite becomes top non‑VXR specification

Final‑year Z19DTH units show improved DPF reliability with updated software
High‑mileage Z22SE examples now dominate replacement enquiries due to age

faq.h2: Vauxhall Signum Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Vauxhall Signum engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Vauxhall Signum engine prices range from £950 for a 1.8L petrol Z18XE to £5,500 for a VXR Z28NET V6 turbo, depending on engine code, condition and supplier. Supply & fit adds £400-£900 labour.
keyPoints: Z19DTH diesel rebuilt: £1,400-£2,800 supply only
Z22SE petrol rebuilt: £1,100-£2,600 supply only
VXR Z28NET specialist rebuild: £2,900-£5,500
All EngineMarket quotes include 12‑month warranty
items.cta: Compare live Vauxhall Signum engine replacement prices today

items.question: What is the most common Vauxhall Signum engine failure?
items.answer: The Z22SE 2.2L petrol engine's timing chain tensioner failure is the most documented Signum issue, typically occurring between 60,000-100,000 miles and causing rattling, misfires or stalling if ignored.
keyPoints: Z22SE tensioner failure affects Life/Design/Elite/Exclusiv trims
Upgraded tensioner kits included in all rebuilt Z22SE units
Early warning: metallic rattle on cold start
Preventative replacement costs £500-£900 at specialists
items.cta: Get quotes for Z22SE timing chain repair or replacement

items.question: Can I replace my Signum engine with a different engine code?
items.answer: Limited interchangeability exists between Signum engine codes due to unique ECU mapping, ancillary brackets and emissions hardware. We verify compatibility before any quote is issued.
keyPoints: Z19DT and Z19DTH share block but differ in EGR/DPF hardware
Z22SE ECU immobiliser requires Tech2 pairing after swap
VXR Z28NET needs performance calibration verification
Always confirm fitment via registration lookup first
items.cta: Check Vauxhall Signum engine compatibility with your VIN

items.question: How long does a Vauxhall Signum engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical turnaround is 3-5 working days for supply only, or 5-7 days for supply & fit including ECU coding and road testing, depending on engine code and garage availability.
keyPoints: Common codes (Z19DTH, Z22SE) often available ex‑stock
VXR Z28NET may require 7-10 days for specialist rebuild
Supply & fit includes Tech2 coding and emissions reset
Nationwide delivery available on all rebuilt units
items.cta: Book Vauxhall Signum engine supply & fit with guaranteed timeline

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Vauxhall Signum engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt Vauxhall Signum engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at any UK garage, with optional extensions available.
keyPoints: Warranty valid UK‑wide, not tied to original supplier
Covers manufacturing defects and premature wear
Requires proof of professional installation and oil change
Claim process handled directly via EngineMarket support
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall Signum engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: Do I need to reprogram the ECU after a Signum engine replacement?
items.answer: Yes - Vauxhall Signum immobiliser systems require Tech2/Op‑Com coding to pair the replacement engine's ECU with the vehicle's security module, a service included in all EngineMarket supply & fit quotes.
keyPoints: Z22SE and Z19DTH ECUs need immobiliser adaptation
VXR Z28NET requires performance calibration verification
Specialist garages in our network have required diagnostic tools
Coding failure can cause no‑start or limp mode post‑install
items.cta: Find Vauxhall Signum specialists with Tech2 coding capability

items.question: Is a used Vauxhall Signum engine reliable?
items.answer: Used Signum engines can be reliable if sourced from low‑mileage, well‑maintained donors with full service history, but rebuilt units with upgraded components offer better long‑term value for high‑mileage vehicles.
keyPoints: Request compression test and service records before purchase
Used engines typically carry 3‑month warranty vs 12‑month on rebuilt
Rebuilt units include upgraded tensioners/seals addressing known failures
Best for Signum values under £2,000 where budget is primary concern
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Vauxhall Signum engine options

items.question: How do I find my Vauxhall Signum engine code?
items.answer: Your Signum engine code is stamped on the engine block near the gearbox flange, listed on the V5C registration document under "engine number", or instantly verified by entering your reg on EngineMarket.
keyPoints: Common codes: Z19DTH (diesel), Z22SE (petrol), Z28NET (VXR)
DVLA lookup via registration confirms exact variant and code
Engine bay sticker may show code but can fade or be removed
Our free tool cross‑references VIN, reg and engine code instantly
items.cta: Identify your Vauxhall Signum engine code in 60 seconds

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Signum engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt Vauxhall Signum engines undergo full disassembly with upgraded internal components replacing worn parts, while reconditioned units are cleaned, inspected and repaired only where necessary without full strip‑down.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: new pistons/bearings/seals, upgraded tensioners, bench tested
Reconditioned: surface refurbishment, gasket replacement, basic testing
Rebuilt recommended for engines over 80k miles or with known failures
Both carry warranty but rebuilt offers longer‑term reliability assurance
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Vauxhall Signum engine prices

items.question: Can I get supply and fit for my Vauxhall Signum engine replacement?
items.answer: Yes - EngineMarket's network includes UK garages familiar with the Signum's unique chassis who offer complete supply & fit service including engine installation, ECU coding, road test and warranty registration.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £400-£900 to engine price depending on code
Includes Tech2/Op‑Com coding, emissions reset and road test
Nationwide coverage with Signum‑experienced specialist garages
Single‑point responsibility simplifies post‑install support queries
items.cta: Book Vauxhall Signum engine supply & fit at a specialist near you
